question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK hooks for any arbitrary ownerId by iterating hookId values in the Collection<Long> parameter, discovering which accounts have fee-modifying hooks attached, and using that information to craft transactions that trigger unintended fee schedule execution paths?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user pass lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() for any target ownerId, thereby enumerating every hook registered to that account and identifying all ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK extension points that intercept CryptoTransfer fee assessment, enabling targeted fee manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Collection<Long> hookIds containing the maximum allowed number of entries (MAX_REPEATED_QUERY_PARAMETERS) with sequential IDs to perform a bulk scan of hook IDs for a given ownerId, identifying which hookIds correspond to active fee-intercepting ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK contracts, and then use that knowledge to predict fee outcomes outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inclusive range semantics of findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() by setting lowerBound equal to upperBound to probe for the existence of a specific hookId on a target account, confirming whether a fee-modifying hook is active without any authentication, leading to targeted fee schedule reconnaissance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user call findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() with a crafted ownerId that resolves to a system account (e.g., treasury or fee collector account), enumerate its ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK hooks, and use the returned contractId fields to identify EVM contracts that implement fee-modifying allow() logic outside of the intended fee schedule design?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Pageable parameter in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to bypass the MAX_LIMIT constraint by manipulating page size or page number, retrieving more hook records than intended and exposing fee-modifying hook configurations that should be rate-limited?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user pass negative or zero hookId values in the Collection<Long> hookIds parameter to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), triggering unexpected query behavior that returns hooks with boundary hookId values, potentially exposing fee-modifying hooks that are not intended to be publicly discoverable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user pass lowerBound greater than upperBound to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(), and if the underlying JPA query does not validate this inversion, cause an empty result or an unintended full-table scan that leaks timing information about the presence of fee-modifying hooks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the GET /api/v1/accounts/{ownerId}/hooks endpoint backed by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to read the adminKey field of any ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK, determining whether the hook's fee-modifying behavior can be altered and planning a social engineering or key-compromise attack to modify transaction fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user use findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to retrieve the contractId of an ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK, then query the contract's EVM bytecode via the contracts API to reverse-engineer the fee-modification logic implemented in the hook's allow() function, enabling the attacker to craft transactions that exploit edge cases in the fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Collection<Long> hookIds with duplicate values to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), causing the underlying SQL IN clause to process duplicates and potentially return duplicate Hook records, leading to incorrect fee calculation if the caller aggregates fee-related data from the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user use the timestampRange field returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify the exact consensus timestamp when a fee-modifying ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK was created or last updated, enabling replay or timing attacks against the fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deleted=false filter absence in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() — noting that unlike HookStorageRepository which filters deleted records, HookRepository does not filter by deleted — to retrieve soft-deleted fee-modifying hooks and use their contractId to invoke stale fee logic that is no longer intended to be active?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve deleted=true hooks via findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() (since no deleted filter is applied), observe that a previously active ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK with fee-modifying behavior has been soft-deleted, and exploit a race condition window between deletion and consensus finality to trigger the hook's fee logic one final time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user use the extensionPoint field (ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK) returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to confirm which accounts have hooks that intercept the allowance phase of CryptoTransfer, then craft a CryptoTransfer that triggers those hooks with a customFee Transfers struct containing extreme values (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE amounts) to cause integer overflow in fee calculation outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user perform a timing side-channel attack by measuring response times of findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() with different ownerId values, inferring which accounts have large numbers of fee-modifying hooks and thus are high-value targets for fee manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PagingAndSortingRepository inheritance of HookRepository to call inherited findAll() or findAll(Pageable) methods that are not explicitly restricted, bypassing the ownerId scoping of findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() and retrieving fee-modifying hooks across all owners?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that PagingAndSortingRepository exposes a findAll(Sort) method to retrieve all Hook records sorted by hookId without any ownerId filter, exposing all fee-modifying ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK configurations across the entire network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user supply an ownerId of 0 or Long.MIN_VALUE to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), causing the query to match hooks with a default or uninitialized ownerId value, potentially exposing system-level fee-modifying hooks that were persisted with invalid owner references?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user use the Sort parameter within the Pageable object passed to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to sort by fields other than hookId (e.g., createdTimestamp or contractId), causing unintended query plans that expose fee-modifying hook data in an order that reveals temporal patterns of fee schedule changes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of rate limiting on findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to perform a high-frequency enumeration of hookIds across all known account ownerIds, building a complete map of fee-modifying ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK deployments on the network and identifying the most impactful targets for fee manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user use the contractId field returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify EVM contracts implementing ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK logic, then call the hook storage API to read the contract's EVM storage slots containing fee parameters (e.g., fee thresholds, collector addresses), enabling precise fee manipulation outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user chain findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() with the hook storage API to read EVM storage slots of a fee-modifying hook contract, discover that the hook's allow() function uses a storage-based fee whitelist, and then craft a transaction that exploits a missing entry in that whitelist to bypass custom fee assessment entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(long ownerId, long lowerBound, long upperBound, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user use the timestampRange lower bound from findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify when a fee-modifying hook was most recently updated, correlate that with on-chain transaction history, and infer what fee parameters were changed, enabling prediction of future fee schedule behavior outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(long ownerId, Collection<Long> hookIds, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the composite Id class (hookId, ownerId) used by HookRepository to perform cross-owner hook ID collision attacks, where the same hookId value exists for multiple ownerIds, and the attacker uses findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to confirm which owner's hook has fee-modifying behavior to target?
